questions.question: What is the main theme of 'Say the Right Thing'?
questions.correct_answer: Etiquette, politeness, and the impact of our words on others
questions.explanation: The chapter focuses on how choosing the right words can build relationships and avoid unnecessary conflict.

questions.question: Why is 'thinking before speaking' emphasized in the lesson?
questions.correct_answer: To ensure that words do not hurt someone's feelings accidentally
questions.explanation: Once spoken, words cannot be taken back; therefore, mindfulness is essential for effective communication.

questions.question: What are the 'magic words' mentioned in the context of polite conversation?
questions.explanation: These words are considered the foundation of good manners and show respect to the listener.

questions.question: How should one handle a disagreement according to the chapter?
questions.correct_answer: By expressing opinions calmly and listening to the other person
questions.explanation: Being 'right' is less important than being respectful during a conflict.

questions.question: What does 'tone of voice' contribute to a message?
questions.correct_answer: It conveys the emotion and intention behind the words
questions.explanation: The same sentence can sound kind or rude depending on the pitch and volume used.

questions.question: What is the consequence of 'saying the wrong thing' at the wrong time?
questions.correct_answer: It can cause misunderstandings, hurt, or embarrassment
questions.explanation: Social blunders often occur when people are insensitive to the context of a situation.

questions.question: What role does empathy play in choosing what to say?
questions.correct_answer: It helps us imagine how our words will make the other person feel
questions.explanation: Empathy acts as a filter that helps us remain kind even in difficult conversations.

questions.question: How can silence be the 'right thing' to say?
questions.correct_answer: Sometimes listening is more powerful than speaking
questions.explanation: In moments of grief or intense anger, providing a listening ear is often the best response.

questions.question: What is the importance of a sincere apology?
questions.correct_answer: It mends broken trust and shows accountability
questions.explanation: Saying 'I'm sorry' effectively requires acknowledging the mistake without making excuses.

questions.question: What does 'tact' mean in this chapter?
questions.correct_answer: The ability to tell the truth without being offensive
questions.explanation: Tact involves being honest while remaining sensitive to the listener’s feelings.

notes.title: The Power of Words
notes.content: Words have the power to heal or to wound; choose them as carefully as you choose your friends.

notes.title: Non-Verbal Cues
notes.content: Body language and facial expressions must match your words for you to be seen as sincere.

notes.title: Active Listening
notes.content: Saying the right thing often starts with listening carefully to what the other person is actually saying.

notes.title: Constructive Criticism
notes.content: When giving feedback, focus on the action rather than the person to keep the conversation positive.

notes.title: The Golden Rule
notes.content: Speak to others in the same way you would like to be spoken to.

facts.fact: Over 70% of communication is non-verbal, meaning how you look is as important as what you say.
A 'timed pause' before answering a difficult question can help you stay in control of your emotions.
Public speaking and social interaction are skills that improve with practice and self-reflection.
